##### Job description

**Wil jij een sleutelrol spelen in de opbouw van een modern dataplatform en ervoor zorgen dat data op een efficiënte en betrouwbare manier beschikbaar wordt gesteld voor analyse en rapportering? Bij Tabaknatie zijn we volop bezig met de implementatie van Microsoft Fabric en zoeken we een Data Engineer om ons team te versterken.**

**Als Data Engineer ben je verantwoordelijk voor de technische ondersteuning van ons Data-team en de verdere uitbouw van onze Fabric-omgeving. Je helpt bij het opzetten en optimaliseren van data pipelines, zorgt voor een stabiele en veilige datastroom en waakt over data governance en kwaliteit. Dankzij jouw werk bouwen we een stevige fundering voor diverse rapportages en analyses binnen Tabaknatie. Van onze logistieke activiteiten tot alle ondersteunende processen.**

## Wat ga je doen?

- Ontwerpen, bouwen en onderhouden van **data pipelines** en **modelleren** van datasets binnen **Microsoft Fabric**.
- **Optimaliseren van de data-infrastructuur** voor betrouwbaarheid, schaalbaarheid en prestaties.
- Beheren en implementeren van **data governance** en waarborgen van **data security** en **kwaliteit**.
- Opvolgen en onderhouden van de **CI/CD-omgeving**.
- Ondersteunen van het **IT en Data-Team** bij diverse **Projecten**.
- Meewerken aan **innovatieve** projecten en nieuwe **technologieën** verkennen binnen **Fabric** en de bredere Microsoft Data Stack.

## Wie zoeken we?

Je bent een zelfstandige en analytische professional met een passie voor data-engineering. Je denkt in oplossingen en hebt een proactieve houding. Je werkt graag samen met verschillende departementen om hun data-noden te vertalen.

Je beschikt ook over:

- Ervaring met **SQL**, **Python**(PySpark) en kennis van **datamodellering** en **ETL**-processen.
- Een scherp oog voor data **governance**, **security** en **compliance**.
- Een **eerste ervaring** met of grote **interesse** in Microsoft
- Ervaring binnen **Power BI,** MS Azure PAAS data platform is een plus.
- Een **zelfstandige**, gestructureerde manier van werken en een sterk **probleemoplossend**

**Waarom voor Tabaknatie werken** **?**

Bij Tabaknatie zal je deel uitmaken van een hechte familie van getalenteerde professionals die elkaar ondersteunen en aanmoedigen om gezamenlijk grenzen te verleggen. We geloven in het creëren van een omgeving waarin iedereen zich gewaardeerd voelt en waar individuele groei en ontwikkeling worden gestimuleerd.

Als wereldleider zijn we voortdurend in beweging, en dat houdt ons werk boeiend en uitdagend. Geen dag is hetzelfde bij Tabaknatie, en dat maakt ons uniek in onze branche. We omarmen verandering en innovatie, waardoor onze medewerkers de kans krijgen om hun vaardigheden te ontwikkelen en nieuwe mogelijkheden te ontdekken.

Kiezen voor een carrière bij Tabaknatie betekent kiezen voor een werkplek waar je je gewaardeerd voelt, waar je kunt groeien en waar je deel uitmaakt van een familie die zich inzet voor succes.

Sluit je aan bij ons team en ervaar zelf waarom onze medewerkers zo trots zijn om bij Tabaknatie te werken.

**Tabaknatie, a family business resulting in one vision with quick decisions, has positioned itself over the years as a global leader in storage, transport, logistics and other related activities around organic and other high-quality products.**

Today Tabaknatie has specialised warehouses with a total surface area of over 1,000,000m², offering the most advanced technologies for storage and handling as well as for your logistics and transport needs. This allows us to offer a wide range of high-quality services to our customers.

At Tabaknatie we deviate from a classic relationship between customer and service provider. We opt for a valuable, respectful and reliable partnership that benefits both parties and we are committed to think along with our clients. Impeccable satisfaction is the goal in which we can offer the flexibility our customers need and this for a 100% service range. We are an established player in our sector and proud of it!

web.markdown:
![Thumbnail (1920x1080)](https://i.ytimg.com/vi/tR80W2mNk2Y/maxresdefault.jpg)
# [The WMS Career Nobody Told You About | $72K to $280K+ Salary Path](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=tR80W2mNk2Y)

**Visibility**: Public
**Uploaded by**: [Chris Schwenk | Tech Jobber Podcast](https://www.youtube.com/@TechJobberPod)
**Uploaded at**: 2026-03-29
**Published at**: 
**Length**: 18:07
**Views**: 2682
**Likes**: 
**Category**: People & Blogs

## Description

```
Think supply chain jobs don't pay? Think again. 💰
In this video, I break down the full Warehouse Management System (WMS) career ladder — from Associate Consultant at $72K all the way to Independent Consultant clearing $280K+ per year. And no, you don't need to be an executive to get there.
What you'll learn:
✅ What Warehouse Management Systems actually are (and why every Amazon & Walmart-scale operation runs on them)
✅ The top WMS platforms to specialize in: Manhattan Associates, Blue Yonder, SAP EWM, Oracle WMS, and Körber
✅ 3 entry paths into WMS — even if you're starting from a warehouse floor
✅ The salary breakdown at every level: Associate → Consultant → Senior → Principal → Independent
✅ How to position yourself for $130–$200/hr contract rates as an independent WMS consultant
✅ Why AI + robotics integration is the next multiplier for WMS professionals
The WMS market is $6B+ and growing at 15% annually — demand for skilled professionals is massively outpacing supply. This is one of the most underrated career moves in supply chain management right now.

🎯 Career Paths Covered:

Direct Consultant Track (vendor-side: Manhattan, Blue Yonder, Körber)
Floor-to-Tech Path (warehouse ops → WMS admin → professional services)
Systems Integrator Route (Deloitte, Accenture, Capgemini supply chain practices)


🔔 Subscribe for weekly tech career breakdowns, salary data, and strategies that actually work.
📌 Chapters:
00:00 – What is a Warehouse Management System?
02:00 – The WMS Market Opportunity
04:00 – Top WMS Vendors to Know
07:00 – The WMS Career Ladder + Salaries
12:00 – 3 Entry Paths Into WMS
17:00 – How to Position Yourself for $280K+
20:00 – AI & Robotics: The Next Multiplier

#WarehouseManagementSystem #WMSCareer #SupplyChainManagement #TechCareers #ManhattanAssociates #BlueYonder #SAPEWM #WarehouseJobs #TechJobberPodcast #CareerAdvice
```

## Transcript

All right, guys. So, right now inside
Amazon's fulfillment center, there is a
software running that knows exactly
where every single item is. Every
pallet, every bin, every shelf. And that
software costs over a million dollars to
implement. The people that are actually
install it, they're making 270k a year.
I'm not talking about software engineers
writing the code. I'm not talking about
data scientists building the models. I'm
talking about a career that lives inside
warehouses, logistics companies, and
Fortune 500 supply chains. And almost
nobody in tech is actually applying for.
So, today we're bringing down the
warehouse management system industry,
the big players, the real money, and
exactly how you can get in.
Now, if you remember my recent interview
with Ken Widner, he was the CIO of Do it
Best Group, their nationwide retail
hardware chain. One thing we briefly
touched on was the supply and demand
issue in warehouse systems people. Let
me give you the foundation first,
because if you've never heard of
warehouse management systems, you'd
understand why this technology exists
before we talk about the money. So,
think about this.
Amazon operates over 1,100 warehouses
globally. Walmart has over 300
distribution centers, Target, Home
Depot, Costco, all of them. Every one of
those facilities has thousands of
products moving in and out every single
day. Now, how does the warehouse know
where everything actually is? How does
it know what to pick first, what lane to
send a pallet down, or when to trigger a
replenishment order? That's the
warehouse management system. It is the
brain inside of the warehouse.
And here's the thing, this isn't a
totally new category either. Warehouse
management systems have been around
since the '80s. But right now in 2026
with e-commerce still exploding, AI
being layered on top of supply chains,
and companies moving from legacy systems
to full cloud platforms, the demand for
people who know this type of software is
at an absolute all-time high. Gartner
actually puts the global WMS market at
over $6 billion. It's growing at 15% a
year. That means companies are spending
big money now, and they need people to
implement that. That means potentially a
great opportunity for you. Now, let's
meet the big players in this space. So,
in this industry there are really five
big names that you need to absolutely
know. These are the companies that are
paying the big salaries that we're
talking about right now, and these are
the logos you want on your LinkedIn for
recruiters like me to see because
there's not that many of these type of
people out there. So, player number one
is Manhattan Associates. Now, I once had
a Manhattan role open for over 6 months,
and we finally found the right guy, and
he failed the background check. His
education was not what he listed. So,
after 6 months of searching for anyone
that was decent enough for the client to
hire,
he didn't end up starting. That's how
low the supply of Manhattan experts is
right now. Also, that job paid over $120
an hour. Now, if warehouse management
systems were a TV show, Manhattan
Associates would be the lead character.
They're the dominant pure play WMS
vendor in the United States today.
Publicly traded at Nasdaq under the
ticker MANH,
and they have been on an absolute run.
Their stock went from about $30 a share
in 2019 to over $200 today. That's kind
of just an example of the demand of
their product, and with that comes a
need for people to implement it. Their
product is called Manhattan Active WMS,
and it runs on the cloud. It's modular.
It's the most widely deployed enterprise
WMS in North America. Companies like The
Home Depot and Dick's Sporting Goods,
they run on Manhattan. This is the name
that carries the most weight on a
resume. In a few minutes, I'm going to
show you exactly how much they're paying
at each level of the career ladder.
Now, player two is Blue Yonder. Blue
Yonder is a global powerhouse in the
space. They're originally JDA Software,
one of the oldest supply chain
technologies in the world, and Panasonic
acquired them in 2021 for $8.5 billion.
Their platform has over 3,000 customers
across 76 countries. Manhattan is the US
king, Blue Yonder is the global force.
If you want international career
exposure, then look into careers in Blue
Yonder. Player three is SAP EWM. As I
covered in a recent video, SAP is the
ERP big dog. If you spent any time in
enterprise tech, then you've probably
heard of SAP. But EWM stands for
extended warehouse management. It's
SAP's native WMS module. And here's why
it matters. If a company already runs
SAP for their ERP, they are very likely
going to standardize on SAP EWM for
their warehouse, too. That means the
install base is absolutely massive. SAP
skills are the most transferable in all
enterprise software, and SAP EWM
consultant can walk into virtually any
Fortune 500 company. Player four is
Oracle WMS. As you probably figured,
that is Oracle's WMS software. It's a
cloud-native warehouse management
product of theirs. It's built into the
Oracle Fusion Cloud Suite, which means
if a company runs Oracle NetSuite, as I
also talked about in a recent video, or
Oracle ERP Cloud, you are likely seeing
Oracle WMS in the warehouse.
Particularly strong in retail, consumer
goods, and third-party logistics
providers. Also growing very fast these
days.
Player five is Körber. This is the one I
want to put on your radar as an
up-and-comer. Körber is a German company
that has been on an acquisition spree in
the supply chain tech space. Their
product is K WMS, and they're growing
extremely fast. Because they're less
established than Manhattan and Blue
Yonder, they're actively building out
their consultant teams. The barrier to
entry currently pretty low.
But the upside is pretty big. So, those
are the big five. You have Manhattan
Associates, Blue Yonder, SAP EWM, Oracle
WMS, and Körber. Now, let's talk about
the reason you probably tuned into this
video, and that is the big money. So,
I'm going to walk you through exactly
what the salary ladder is in this
industry. Now, I want you to understand,
these are not inflated numbers. These
are pulled from my recruitment business
with some of them, and other
technologies I haven't been able to work
with yet, I pulled from LinkedIn and
Glassdoor. So, here's how the ladder
currently looks. So, rung one, you have
the associate consultant. So, this is
basically your entry point for all of
these companies. This is where you are
fresh, you just got your degree. Could
be IT, computer science, maybe business,
industrial engineering, supply chains.
They hire a lot of those type of people.
Doesn't really matter. At that level,
you're just learning the software.
You're kind of just sitting back and
helping on these implementations
wherever you can. Maybe building some
test scripts, doing some data migration
support. You're basically in the room
when it happens, but you're not really
running the room yet. Now, for these
type of roles at Manhattan Associates,
uh you're looking around 72 to 90k.
Certainly not life-changing money, but
you're getting paid to learn a skill set
that really very few people have. Now,
rung two is the consultant. So, you've
gotten a few years of some specific WMS
platform experience, and you're running
your own work streams on implementation
projects.
You're in the client meetings, you're
configuring the system, and you're not
just supporting it. This is where the
compensation does jump up. For Manhattan
Associates, this is where the average
consultant gets 127k.
I've seen in the recruitment business, I
deal with more hourly roles, and these
are at 80 to 90 an hour, which is
obviously a lot more than that. Now, at
Blue Yonder, the range is nearly
identical to Manhattan. You're looking
at 90 to 145k
when you get to that consultant level.
Total comp including bonuses and travel
per diem, cuz you're probably going to
be traveling, can push you over 160k
right to that 80 to 90 an hour that I
was talking about. Then we have rung
three. That's the senior consultant or
principal consultant. This is where the
money gets real. So, a principal
consultant at Manhattan Associates,
they're going to pay them around 275k,
maybe up to 300. Now, from the hourly
perspective, I've seen 150 up to 200 an
hour, which kind of equates to like 400k
a year.
So, that is big-time money considering
you're not a VP, you're not an
executive. You're basically a consultant
with very specialized knowledge of the
software platform that all the world's
biggest retail companies cannot function
without. But with that money does come
the pressure, because at this level,
you're basically running the full
implementation program. You are the
subject matter expert in the room, and
they are putting a lot of money to bring
Manhattan in and pay you. So, when a $50
WMS implementation hits a critical
problem,
you're going to be getting some phone
calls from some very high-level people
at this level. So, on the SAP side,
those functional consultants, from what
I've seen, it's kind of in the 145 to
150k, and the contract rates obviously
always are going to push that a bit
higher, maybe to 90 to 100 an hour.
Okay, so round four, you have the
program manager or director. Now, this
is the top of the ladder inside the
vendor organizations.
So, you're looking at 200 to 242 in
base, but here's a number that I
actually want to give you cuz obviously
I'm more focused on the hourly rates.
So, the independent consultant rate for
these roles, once you have 7 to 10 years
kind of focused experience on a
Manhattan or an SAP type program, so you
can go independent. Now, I have seen WMS
consultants at 130 to 200 per hour
paid to their LLC.
I have also seen it go up to 250 an
hour. Obviously, we know that is 500k.
So, at this level, you're a hired gun,
maybe you're able to bring a couple
people under you on the project, and
you're minting some big-time money.
Sometimes, I've even seen these guys
take on multiple clients at once billing
these insane rates. Because once things
are fully implemented, they may keep you
on for maintenance. Maybe it's a few
hours a week, but you add that up over a
few clients, and you're still minting a
ton of money. Okay, so you might be
saying, all right, shut up, the money's
big, competition is low.
How the hell do I get into this thing?
So, here's how you actually get a start.
I'm going to give you three paths here.
Maybe one of them fits where you are
right now. Path one, we have the direct
consultant track. So, this is the
traditional entry point here, okay? So,
you got your bachelor's degree, like we
said, business supply chain, maybe IT
engineering, stuff like that. Anything
vaguely technical, you can get a start
here. So, here's what you do. You get
your WMS certification, not a generic
supply chain cert. I mean,
platform-specific training. Manhattan
Associates even has online training
programs for you. And I have seen
third-party providers like ProExcellency
offer Manhattan WMS certification
courses for around 500 to 2,000 dollars.
I don't have any connection to them, but
check these guys out. This is the best
one I could find. So, that's a pretty
good price for a career that pays
quickly into the six figures. Once you
get that, you go on LinkedIn and search
for associate consultant WMS,
implementation analyst, supply chain,
and I just looked, there are over 470
open entry-level roles in this space.
So, you're applying to everything.
You're applying directly to Manhattan,
Blue Yonder, Körber, SAP, Oracle,
and also the systems integrators that
implement their software. So, you're
looking at some consulting firms,
Deloitte, Accenture, Capgemini. It's
basically degree plus certification
quickly, and then get your applications
in. That's the most direct path. Then
you have path two, which is the
floor-to-tech path. So, this one is
underrated, but it is genuinely powerful
in this space. You ever worked in a
distribution center, a third-party
logistics company, a retail backroom,
you have something that most consultants
in this space don't have. You have
actually used the softwares. You know
what it feels like when a pick path is
wrong. You know what a mislabeled pallet
does to an outbound shipment. You
understand a factory floor. And these
companies always love consultants who
have operational experience with the
software. It gives you an added
credibility that the certification
course cannot teach. The move here would
be get into a warehouse tech role, maybe
WMS admin, maybe an inventory systems
analyst, maybe a WMS coordinator.
Basically, learn the platform from the
inside, then lateral into the
professional services at one of those
big vendors. This path does take a bit
longer, but your career ceiling is often
higher and can go exponentially faster
because you know the software better.
And path three, we have the systems
integrator route.
So, SI stands for systems integrator.
These are again the large consulting
firms that get hired to implement WMS
platforms on behalf of their enterprise
clients. Think Deloitte supply chain,
Accenture operations, Capgemini
Intelligent Industry. These firms have
entire WMS practices, and they hire
junior consultants with very limited
direct WMS experience, and pretty much
just train them on the job. Because of
this, starting pay is a bit lower than
going direct to a vendor, but the upside
is incredible experience. You'll see 10,
15, 20 different implementations across
multiple industries in your first 3
years. Now, you've kind of seen how
these things break, and that breadth of
experience builds your value faster than
any other path. Then at year three or
four, you have your pick of the
opportunities. You go direct to a
vendor, or you go independent and keep
climbing inside the systems integrator.
Now, I want to give you one more layer
because knowing the companies and the
yourself for this career is what
actually gets you hired. So, here's what
WMS hiring managers actually look for.
So, number one is platform specificity.
Don't just put supply chain software on
your resume.
Name the platform and how you used it in
every possible way. Was it Manhattan,
Blue Yonder, SAP, Oracle, Körber?
Obviously, if you have any of those
certifications, let's see that at the
very top of the resume and your
LinkedIn. Number two is implementation
life cycle experience. So, WMS
consulting is project-based work. Hiring
managers want to know if you understand
what an implementation actually looks
like.
Between the blueprinting, configuration,
user acceptance testing, cutover, the
go-live support, even if your experience
is limited, you need to speak these
terms and in this language.
Number three is the intersection play.
Here's where I want you to actually
think bigger.
The most valuable consultants in this
space sit at the intersection of WMS and
emerging technologies. Specifically,
AI-driven demand forecasting,
robotics integration, that's going to be
obviously huge in the next few years,
and the automation software is already
in place. So, companies like Manhattan
Associates are building AI directly into
their platform like everyone else is. If
you can walk into a room and understand
WMS layer, and I understand how the AI
optimization layer sits on top of it,
well, you're talking a completely
different stratosphere there. That is
the play that gets you from 145k to that
270k that I talked about in the
beginning faster than just grinding out
years and years of experience.
Okay, so let's land the plane here. WMS
is a $6 billion
industry growing at 15% a year. The
career has a defined ladder, associate
consultant to principal to independent
contractor, with real documented
compensation at every rung. The big
names are, once again, Manhattan
Associates, Blue Yonder, SAP, EWM,
Oracle WMS, and the new Körber.
Pick one platform, go deep on it, get
your certification as quickly as
possible, and start positioning now. The
people making 270k in this space aren't
any smarter than you, they just found a
career where the demand is skyrocketing,
supply of skilled workers is, and I can
attest to this, extremely low, and
almost nobody on YouTube has even told
you it exists.
Until today.
And if you haven't subscribed yet,
please do that and help us out. We are
highlighting all the niche tech skills
that are tough to fill in my recruitment
business that you should be considering,
and how to get into it, and how much
they pay.
Thanks. We'll catch you in the next one.

## What is an Infor WMS job?

An Infor WMS job typically involves working with Infor Warehouse Management System (WMS) software to optimize warehouse operations, inventory management, and supply chain efficiency. Professionals in this role may be responsible for system configuration, troubleshooting, training users, and integrating Infor WMS with other enterprise systems. They often work in logistics, retail, or manufacturing industries to ensure seamless warehouse automation. Strong knowledge of warehouse processes, database management, and system customization is usually required.

## What are the key skills and qualifications needed to thrive in the Infor Wms position, and why are they important?

To thrive as an Infor WMS specialist, a strong background in warehouse management processes, logistics, and supply chain operations is essential, often supported by experience with enterprise resource planning (ERP) systems. Familiarity with Infor WMS software, proficiency in configuration and integration, and certifications such as Infor WMS Certified Specialist are highly valued. Strong analytical thinking, effective communication, and problem-solving abilities help professionals collaborate with cross-functional teams and address operational issues. These skills are vital for optimizing warehouse performance, ensuring accurate system implementation, and maintaining smooth supply chain operations.

## What are some common challenges faced by Infor WMS specialists in their daily work?

Infor WMS specialists often face challenges related to system configuration, integration with other enterprise software, and troubleshooting unexpected data or process issues. They must ensure that warehouse operations run smoothly while adapting the system to evolving business requirements, which can be complex in fast-paced environments. Collaborating with IT, operations, and end-users is essential for identifying root causes, customizing workflows, and delivering solutions that balance efficiency and accuracy. Over time, professionals in this role develop expertise in balancing technical demands with operational needs, making them invaluable to organizations seeking to streamline their supply chain.

## Job description

Description:

**Join the Pack at Pet Food Experts!**

With a rich history spanning over 80 years, Pet Food Experts has evolved from a small family-run business into the nation's leading pet specialty distributor, proudly serving over 10,000 pet retail locations nationwide.

At Pet Food Experts (PFX), we do more than distribute the best pet products—we fuel independent pet retailers with passion, expertise, and an unwavering commitment to community. And now, with unprecedented growth and an expanded national footprint, we’re stronger than ever, ready to serve more retailers, support more brands, and make an even bigger impact in the pet industry.

If you're an animal lover with a heart for independent businesses and a drive for excellence, we want you on our team!

**What You'll Do:**

ORDER SELECTORS, EXPEDITORS AND BREAKDOWN ASSOCIATES

Our Warehouse Teams accurately replenish product or fulfill customer orders by selecting, moving and loading products (e.g. bags of dog/cat food, cases of cans, litter) from warehousing racks and storage areas onto pallets both manually and operating warehouse equipment.

- This position requires the candidate to be assigned to the walk-in freezer. This requires the ability to work in temperatures at or below -5 degrees F for long periods of time while wearing required personal protective equipment provided by the company
- Safely and solely bend, lift, move materials up to 50 lbs. repetitively and continuously throughout shift, manually and by operating warehouse equipment
- Build pallets accurately, properly, and efficiently to ensure product is not damaged upon delivery to our customers
- Perform assigned functions, accountabilities and supporting activities while complying with safety procedures, rules and regulations

**What We're Looking For:**

- Experience in safely operating warehouse equipment (e.g. forklift, pallet jack, high reach truck), preferred
- Candidate must have reliable transportation, flexible schedule, solid attendance and a positive, professional attitude
- Strong attention to detail and accuracy required
- High degree of skill and dexterity in operation and safe control of warehouse equipment
- Experience and certification for an electric pallet jack, a plus
- Experience with Infor WMS, a plus

**Why You'll Love Working Here:**

Pet People, First and Always: We’re a company built on relationships—both with our team and the independent pet community we serve.

- An Industry on the Move: With our recent expansion, we’re more connected than ever, creating career growth opportunities at every level.
- Culture of Care: From employee wellness to professional development, we invest in YOU.
- Fun & Furry Perks: Think company events, supportive colleagues, and a community that actually cares.

**Schedule:** Monday- Friday: 2:00 pm- work is complete

**Application Deadline:** Ongoing

**Compensation: Step Progression Pay System**

At Pet Food Experts, we believe in recognizing the commitment and dedication of our team. That's why we've designed a pay structure to support your long-term growth and reward high performance.

**Here’s how it works:**

- **Starting Pay:** $18 per hour.
- **Guaranteed Pay Increases:** Employees receive a pay increase after 6 months of employment and an additional increase after 1 year of service and beyond
- **Additional Earning Opportunities:** Pay rate increases are scheduled regularly based on your length of service

**Pawsome Perks of Joining PFX:**

- No Scheduled Weekends! – More time for family, adventures, or just lounging with your pets.
- Full Benefits Package – Medical, dental, and vision to keep you feeling your best.
- 401(k) with Employer Match – Because your future matters.
- Safety Footwear Reimbursement – We help keep your feet comfy and protected!
- Overtime Pay – Extra $$ for extra hours (because your time is valuable).
- Paid Holidays – Celebrate, relax, and recharge—on us!
- Employee Discounts on Pet Food & Supplies – Because your pets deserve the best, too!

**At PFX, we take care of our team—because when you feel good, you do good. Ready to be part of something bigger? Apply today!**

\*Any offers of employment are contingent upon passing a required background check

**Ready to join the pack?** Apply today.

**Want to go beyond the job description?** Follow @LifeatPFX to explore employee spotlights, real team moments, and an authentic look at how our culture comes to life across our network.

**Pet Food Experts is an equal opportunity employer. Pet Food Experts does not discriminate in employment on account of race, color, religion, national origin, citizenship status, ancestry, age, sex (including sexual harassment), sexual orientation, marital status, physical or mental disability, military status or unfavorable discharge from military service.**

web.markdown:
[Skip to content](https://ntg.com/job-listing/senior-program-manager-group-wms-transformation/#content)

[![NTG](https://ntg.com/wp-content/uploads/ntg-logo.png)](https://ntg.com/)

[Link to https://ntg.com/global-locations/](https://ntg.com/global-locations/)

![Job listing](https://ntg.com/wp-content/plugins/ntg-job-board/single-default-res.png)

# Senior Program Manager – Group WMS Transformation

Ready to design the warehouse blueprint a Europeanlogisticsgroup will scale on?

Added: 02 June 2026

Where others see racking, workflows and busy loading bays, you see patterns, structure and potential. You know how to turn local best practices into a unified model - and how to design operations that don’t just work today but continue to work when volumes double and sites multiply.

NTG Road & Logistics is looking for a strategic and experienced Senior Program Manager to lead our Group WMS Transformation initiative across Europe: someone who thrives where warehouse operations meet technology, where processes meet reality, and where a growing European logistics group needs a blueprint for the next level.

You will lead one of NTG's most important operational transformation initiatives, defining the future warehouse operating model and driving the selection, implementation and rollout of a group-wide WMS platform across multiple countries and entities.

If you thrive in environments where decisions move quickly, where variety is the norm, and where your work shapes how an entire organisation operates - this may be the role you’ve been preparing for.

**The Group WMS Transformation Program**

NTG Road & Logistics is at a defining moment. Our European footprint is growing fast through acquisitions and expansion, and we now need to shift from many local warehouse setups to one shared, scalable way of working.

To unlock the next level, we must build a unified warehouse model that aligns processes, systems and operations across countries - and this is where you come in.

As Senior Program Manager, you will set the direction for how NTG’s warehouses operate going forward: defining the operating model, leading the selection of a groupwide WMS platform, and preparing the foundation for a structured rollout across entities and countries.

Reporting to the Head of BCM & Integrations, you will work closely with Group IT, Finance, Operations and local management teams across Europe. Based at NTG’s headquarters in Hvidovre, Copenhagen, this is a role with broad influence - and real ownership.

**Your mission**

You will lead the transformation program from strategy and design through vendor selection, implementation planning and future rollout preparation:

- Define NTG’s future warehouse operating model

- Standardise core warehouse processes across entities

- Lead the selection and implementation of a groupwide WMS platform

- Ensure strong alignment with TMS, Finance and Integrations

- Build a scalable capability that works across multiple locations

- Prepare the foundation and roadmap for future rollout

- Establish program governance and decision-making structures

- Manage vendor selection and strategic supplier relationships

- Drive stakeholder alignment across countries and business units

- Develop rollout methodology and deployment roadmap

- Define standards, KPIs and performance measures for warehouse operations

- Lead change management and adoption activities

This role isn’t about maintaining or optimising. It’s about designing the model, selecting the systems, and ensuring every future site benefits from your decisions.

**The profile that can make it happen**

You bring 7–15 years of experience leading logistics, supply chain or technology-enabled transformation initiatives. You have successfully driven complex programs involving operating model design, process standardization, systems implementation and organizational change across multiple sites, business units or countries.

You understand how operations, technology and organizational change interact, and you can align stakeholders ranging from warehouse managers and operational teams to IT, Finance and senior leadership.

You have led large-scale WMS, operational excellence or supply chain transformation programs and understand the challenges of creating alignment across diverse entities, countries and operating models. You know how to challenge legacy setups without disrupting the flow, and you’re quick at turning business needs into clear processes and system requirements.

You are a strong program leader who thrives in ambiguity and can create structures where none exists. You know how to build alignment, establish governance, manage competing priorities and drive decisions in complex stakeholder environments.

**Ready to shape the warehouse model of tomorrow?**

This is your opportunity to shape how NTG designs and scales its warehouse operations for years to come. You will not receive a readymade manual. You will create it.

If this sounds like the strategic operational challenge you’ve been looking for, we would be pleased to hear from you. Send us your application and CV - and tell us why you are the right person to lead NTG’s Warehouse & WMS Transformation.

[APPLY FOR THE JOB](https://ntggroupas.reqruiting.com/Pages/Application/Apply.aspx?id=27453)

metadata.ogDescription: Job Description Summary Senior Oracle EBS WMS OutboundApplication Architect responsible for defining strategy and leading end-to-end architecture, design governance, and delivery for Oracle WMS inbound distribution processes. Partners closely with Product Management to translate vision into clear requirements, align stakeholders (Product Managers, Technical Anchors, Product Owners, Developers, Operations, IT, and vendors), and guide delivery by balancing new features, defect resolution, and technical debt. Establishes scalable, supportable solution patterns and standards across the full lifecycle from discovery and design through build, integration, testing, cutover, hyper care, and steady-state support, enabling reliable execution and repeatable rollout to additional sites. Job Description Roles and Responsibilities Demonstrate superior product and industry knowledge and helps organization to gain the competitive edge. Interacts with customers/stakeholders in VOC requirements-gathering, fine tuning, and obtaining product feedback. Works with cross-functional teams to deliver features and major, complex products. Possesses a deep understanding of the technology stack and impact on final product. Routinely collaborates with UX, Architecture and engineering teams on multiple issues and decisions. Conducts customer and stakeholder interviews and elaborates on personas. Demonstrates expert persuasion and influencing skills that ensure alignment between customer, product manager and engineering teams. Owns the release and sprint backlogs short-term roadmap for MVPs and quarterly releases. Owns and manages the backlog; continuously orders and prioritizes to ensure that 1-2 sprints/iterations of backlog are always ready. Prioritize continuously in accordance with the understanding and validation of customer problems and needs. Demonstrates strategic expert level skills in problem decomposition and ability to navigate through ambiguity. Engages frequently (50% of the time) with the development team; facilitates discussions, provides clarification, story acceptance and refinement, testing and validation; Translates unstructured or ambiguous work requests into actionable user stories and work units. Partners with Development Leadership to ensure healthy development process. Mentors junior team members. Provide technical leadership to TPMs across organization. Expert in Agile Methodology: Coaches others. Ensure design and implementation of Oracle WMS software Define overall architecture, conduct end-user training. Develop and execute test plans, conduct CRP, SIT and UAT. Work with third party integrations such as Parcel Carrier integrations and LTL/TL freight management application. Provide training on how a facility is configured in WMS to know the WMS system function Develop WMS label printing and Web Report tools Implement Supply -Chain-Management solutions and WMS application impact on these processors Support continuous -business-process-improvement projects. Support WMS quarterly system upgrades. Support and documentation of processors and incidents for fanatical audit controls Independently manage new WMS site implementation projects Independently manage project budgets, human capital and project funds Work on WMS application integration, including the integration to the ERP application and build future enhancements. Minimum Qualification Bachelor’s degree from accredited university or college with minimum of 5 years of professional experience OR Associates degree with minimum of 8 years of professional experience OR High School Diploma with minimum of 10 years of professional experience Minimum 5 years of professional experience in implementing WMS functionalities Note: Military experience is equivalent to professional experience Eligibility Requirement: Legal authorization to work in the U.S. is required. We will not sponsor individuals for employment visas, now or in the future, for this job. Preferred Qualifications Technical Expertise: 8+ years of experience in implementing WMS functionalities such as Oracle Shipping, Wave Planning, Pick-pack-and-ship, Load Planning, Shipping documentation, Replenishment, Product Receiving (PO/IB Shipments), Putaway, Slotting, Cross docking, Labeling etc. Business Acumen: Demonstrates the initiative to explore alternate technology and approaches to solving problems Skilled in breaking down problems, documenting problem statements and estimating efforts Has the ability to analyze impact of technology choices Skilled in negotiation to align stakeholders and communicate a single synthesized perspective to the scrum team. Balances value propositions for competing stakeholders. Demonstrates knowledge of the competitive environment Demonstrates knowledge of technologies in the market to help make buy vs build recommendations, scope MVPs, and to drive market timing decisions Leadership: Influences through others; builds direct and "behind the scenes" support for ideas. Pre-emptively sees downstream consequences and effectively tailors influencing strategy to support a positive outcome. Able to verbalize what is behind decisions and downstream implications. Continuously reflecting on success and failures to improve performance and decision-making. Understands when change is needed. Participates in technical strategy planning. Personal Attributes: Able to effectively direct and mentor others in critical thinking skills. Proactively engages with cross-functional teams to resolve issues and design solutions using critical thinking and analysis skills and best practices. Finds important patterns in seemingly unrelated information. Influences and energizes other toward the common vision and goal. Maintains excitement for a process and drives to new directions of meeting the goal even when odds and setbacks render one path impassable. Innovates and integrates new processes and/or technology to significantly add value to GE. Identifies how the cost of change weighs against the benefits and advises accordingly. Proactively learns new solutions and processes to address seemingly unanswerable problems. Additional Information: The base pay range for this position is $131,000.00 - 174,000.00 USD annually. The specific pay offered may be influenced by a variety of factors, including the candidate’s experience, education, and skill set. This position is also eligible for an annual discretionary bonus based on a percentage of your base salary/ commission based on the plan. This posting is expected to close on June 9th, 2026. GE Aerospace offers comprehensive benefits and programs to support your health and, along with programs like HealthAhead, your physical, emotional, financial and social wellbeing. Healthcare benefits include medical, dental, vision, and prescription drug coverage; access to a Health Coach from GE Aerospace; and the Employee Assistance Program, which provides 24/7 confidential assessment, counseling and referral services. Retirement benefits include the GE Aerospace Retirement Savings Plan, a 401(k) savings plan with company matching contributions and company retirement contributions, as well as access to Fidelity resources and planning consultants. Other benefits include tuition assistance, adoption assistance, paid parental leave, disability insurance, life insurance, and paid time-off for vacation or illness. GE Aerospace (General Electric Company or the Company) and its affiliates each sponsor certain employee benefit plans or programs (i.e., is a “Sponsor”). Each Sponsor reserves the right to terminate, amend, suspend, replace or modify its benefit plans and programs at any time and for any reason, in its sole discretion. No individual has a vested right to any benefit under a Sponsor’s welfare benefit plan or program. This document does not create a contract of employment with any individual. Additional Information GE Aerospace offers a great work environment, professional development, challenging careers, and competitive compensation. GE Aerospace is an Equal Opportunity Employer. Employment decisions are made without regard to race, color, religion, national or ethnic origin, sex, sexual orientation, gender identity or expression, age, disability, protected veteran status or other characteristics protected by law. GE Aerospace will only employ those who are legally authorized to work in the United States for this opening. Any offer of employment is conditioned upon the successful completion of a drug screen (as applicable). Relocation Assistance Provided: No #LI-Remote - This is a remote position At GE Aerospace, we invent the future of flight, lift people up, and bring them home safely. We are a world-leading provider of jet engines, components, and integrated systems for commercial and military aircraft. At GE Aerospace, respect for people is core to our culture and commitment to FLIGHT DECK, our proprietary lean operating model. We attract, develop, and retain industry-leading talent from all backgrounds and cultivate communities where people are respected and empowered to reach their fullest potential. For the people of GE Aerospace, their work isn't just a job. It's their passion and purpose. We work and win as one team, accountable to each other and our customers, and we are always finding a better way to climb higher together. We were meant to fly.
